evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my .NET for Android app. Here's my empty Activity: EmptyActivity.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes, show a rectangle highlight on each tracked barcode, and log each newly-tracked barcode's data to the console.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete BarcodeAr integration code into EmptyActivity.cs (new BarcodeAr(context, settings), BarcodeArView.Create(...) with a ViewGroup parent (FrameLayout or layout root), IBarcodeArHighlightProvider implementation that returns Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?> with a BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode), SessionUpdated event handler that iterates session.NewlyTrackedBarcodes and logs each barcode's Data via RunOnUiThread, barcodeArView.OnPause/OnResume lifecycle, barcodeArView.Start() after providers, Dispose on teardown), and shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, AndroidManifest permission, runtime permission, MainApplication initialize for SDK 8.0+, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding android.permission.CAMERA to AndroidManifest.xml
Setup checklist mentions requesting the CAMERA permission at runtime
Setup checklist mentions creating a MainApplication subclass that calls ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() (required for SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions setting a Theme.AppCompat descendant (Theme = @style/Theme.AppCompat...) on the [Activity] attribute
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new BarcodeArSettings() is used (not BarcodeArSettings.Create(), which does not exist)
new BarcodeAr(context, settings) is used as the constructor (not BarcodeAr.Create(...) or BarcodeAr.ForDataCaptureContext(...), which do not exist)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Qr is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Qr are enabled
Kotlin-style underscore symbology names (Symbology.EAN13_UPCA, Symbology.QR) are NOT used
BarcodeArView.Create( is called (not new BarcodeArView(...), which is not a public constructor)
BarcodeArView.Create( receives a ViewGroup (FrameLayout or activity layout root) as the parentView argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives the BarcodeAr instance as the barcodeAr argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives the DataCaptureContext as the dataCaptureContext argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives a BarcodeArViewSettings as the settings argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives null (or a CameraSettings) as the cameraSettings argument
IBarcodeArHighlightProvider is implemented
HighlightForBarcodeAsync is implemented and returns a Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?> (the async/Task-based provider, not a callback)
A new BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context argument is passed)
callback.OnData(...) / callback.onData(...) is NOT used (that is the Kotlin pattern, not the .NET pattern)
barcodeArView.HighlightProvider is assigned to the provider instance
Either barcodeAr.SessionUpdated += handler or barcodeAr.AddListener(IBarcodeArListener) is wired
OnObservationStarted and OnObservationStopped are NOT declared (the .NET IBarcodeArListener has only one method)
barcodeArView.Start() is called after providers are assigned
The activity's OnResume forwards to barcodeArView.OnResume()
The activity's OnPause forwards to barcodeArView.OnPause()
barcodeArView.Dispose() is called on teardown (NOT barcodeArView.OnDestroy(), which does not exist in the .NET binding)
No call to BarcodeCapture.Create or barcodeCapture.AddListener appears (BarcodeAr is not BarcodeCapture)
No DataCaptureView, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, or BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is created (BarcodeArView is the only view needed)
No Camera.GetDefaultCamera() or dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(...) call is present (BarcodeArView manages its own camera internally) (semantic)
No SparkScanCoordinatorLayout is referenced (that is SparkScan-specific and not used by BarcodeAr)

evals.prompt: Add an info annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR .NET Android Activity. The annotation should show the barcode's data as a single body row. Here is my Activity: IntegratedActivity.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider implementation that returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>, constructs a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) with a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent whose Text is barcode.Data, and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider while preserving existing integration.

assertions.text: IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider is implemented
AnnotationForBarcodeAsync is implemented and returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>
A new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context)
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent is used and its Text is set to barcode.Data
The BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent is imported from the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Info sub-namespace
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the new provider instance
callback.OnData(...) is NOT used (the .NET annotation provider is async/Task-based, not callback-based)
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeAr feedback in IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeAr.Feedback to a new BarcodeArFeedback() (the empty constructor, which produces a silent feedback), preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: barcodeAr.Feedback is reassigned to a new BarcodeArFeedback() (empty constructor)
BarcodeArFeedback is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.Feedback
BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback is NOT called as a method (BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback() with parentheses) — it is a static property in .NET
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app, I want each rectangle highlight to use a custom green brush and show a checkmark icon inside it. Update the highlight provider in IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill customizes the BarcodeArRectangleHighlight returned by the existing provider: it sets highlight.Brush to a new Brush(...) and highlight.Icon to a ScanditIcon built with ScanditIconBuilder (WithIcon(ScanditIconType.Checkmark)...Build()), keeping the BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) constructor (Barcode only, no Context) and the async Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?> provider shape.

assertions.text: The provider still constructs a new BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) with only the Barcode argument (no Context)
highlight.Brush is assigned to a new Brush(...) instance
Brush is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Style
highlight.Icon is assigned to a ScanditIcon produced by a ScanditIconBuilder
new ScanditIconBuilder() is used and its Build() method is called to produce the icon
ScanditIconBuilder.WithIcon(ScanditIconType.Checkmark) is called
ScanditIconBuilder and ScanditIconType are imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Icon
The provider still returns Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?> (the async provider shape is preserved)
callback.OnData(...) is NOT used (the .NET highlight provider is async/Task-based, not callback-based)

evals.prompt: Add a status-icon annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app (IntegratedActivity.cs). It should show a checkmark icon, expand to the text 'In stock' when tapped, and show the tip pointing at the barcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that returns a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode), sets its Icon to a ScanditIcon built via ScanditIconBuilder (WithIcon(ScanditIconType.Checkmark)), its Text to "In stock", and HasTip to true, then assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider while preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider is implemented
AnnotationForBarcodeAsync is implemented and returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>
A new BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode) is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context)
BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations
The annotation's Icon is assigned a ScanditIcon built with new ScanditIconBuilder().WithIcon(ScanditIconType.Checkmark)
The annotation's Text is set to "In stock"
The annotation's HasTip is set to true
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the new provider instance
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want a popover with two action buttons (a 'Pick' button and a 'Skip' button) on each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app. Update IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that builds a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, buttons) where buttons is an IList<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton> containing two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton(icon, text) entries (ScanditIcon via ScanditIconBuilder), and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider while preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider is implemented and AnnotationForBarcodeAsync returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>
A new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, buttons) is constructed with the Barcode and an IList<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton> (no Context argument)
Two BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton instances are constructed, one with the text "Pick" and one with the text "Skip"
Each BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton is constructed with a ScanditIcon as the first argument and a string text as the second argument
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Popover
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the new provider instance
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app (IntegratedActivity.cs), I want the annotation to look different depending on how close the barcode is: a detailed info annotation when it's close-up and a compact one when it's far away, switching at 10% of the screen area.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that builds two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) instances (close-up and far-away) and wraps them in a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, closeUpAnnotation, farAwayAnnotation), sets the static BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold to 0.1f, and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider while preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: A new BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, closeUpAnnotation, farAwayAnnotation) is constructed with the Barcode and two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation arguments (no Context)
Two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) instances are constructed to serve as the close-up and far-away variations
BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold is assigned the value 0.1f
BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold is set as a static member on the type (not as an instance property on a constructed annotation)
BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the new provider instance
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: When the user taps a highlight in my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app, I want to log the tapped barcode's data. Wire that up in IntegratedActivity.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill subscribes to the barcodeArView.HighlightForBarcodeTapped C# event (the .NET BarcodeArView has no UiListener property), reads args.Barcode.Data inside the handler, and marshals UI work via RunOnUiThread, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: The handler is wired with barcodeArView.HighlightForBarcodeTapped +=
The handler reads the tapped barcode's data via the event args' Barcode.Data property
A UiListener property is NOT assigned on BarcodeArView (the .NET binding exposes the tap as the HighlightForBarcodeTapped event, not a UiListener property)
IBarcodeArViewUiListener is NOT implemented (the .NET binding uses the HighlightForBarcodeTapped event instead of a UI listener interface)
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: I'm showing info annotations on tracked barcodes in my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app. I want the whole annotation to be tappable and to log the barcode when it's tapped. Update IntegratedActivity.cs to add an info annotation with a tap handler.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider returning a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode), sets EntireAnnotationTappable to true, assigns a Listener implementing IBarcodeArInfoAnnotationListener whose OnInfoAnnotationTapped handles the tap, and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider while preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: A new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no Context)
The annotation's EntireAnnotationTappable property is set to true
The annotation's Listener property is assigned an object implementing IBarcodeArInfoAnnotationListener
IBarcodeArInfoAnnotationListener is implemented with an OnInfoAnnotationTapped(BarcodeArInfoAnnotation annotation) method
IBarcodeArInfoAnnotationListener is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Info
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the new provider instance
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR .NET Android app I add status-icon annotations to tracked barcodes (IntegratedActivity.cs). By default they appear as soon as the barcode is scanned, but I only want each annotation to appear when the user taps its highlight. How do I change that?
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the annotation's AnnotationTrigger property to BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap (instead of the default HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan), preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: The annotation's AnnotationTrigger property is assigned BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTap
BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations
The skill explains that the default trigger is BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved
